features.behavior: Integrations foundation on the server: (1) config module reading/writing ~/.solaris/config.json with 0600 perms holding Exa key, per-mode consents, agent permission mode, default model, addons state; corrupt file yields defaults plus a logged warning, never a crash. (2) Tool detection for qmd and opencode probing known install locations (~/.bun/bin, opencode installer bin) plus a login-shell PATH, with an injectable command runner so tests never depend on real binaries; results cached in memory, re-checkable. (3) GET /api/integrations returns per-tool state (installed, version, configured, connected) and booleans only for secrets, never key material. (4) Security middleware on all routes: Host/Origin validation rejecting foreign hosts, plus a per-session token delivered to the app page and required as a custom header on every mutating or spending route. (5) tsconfig.json include gains bin and desktop so typecheck covers them.

verification.expected: All vitest suites pass including new server/integrations/config.test.ts and detect.test.ts (absent/present/configured states from faked runner, 600 perms, no key in status payload, corrupt config -> defaults, foreign Host rejected, mutating request without token rejected / with token succeeds); typecheck passes now covering bin/ and desktop/

features.context_note: Follow createApp() inline route closures with try/catch 500s (server/app.ts) and the per-vault data dir convention in bin/cli.ts. KTD5 (config), KTD7 (detection), KTD12 (origin+token middleware).
features.evidence: npm test: 32/32 pass (config 6, detect 6, api 7 incl. foreign-Host 403 on all routes, token gate, key never in payload, 600 perms, corrupt->defaults, refresh re-probe); typecheck green now covering bin/ and desktop/
features.verified_by: harness-progress verify (npm test && npm run typecheck, exit 0)

features.behavior: Three icon mode buttons with name tooltips (Semantic/qmd, Web/Exa, Agent/OpenCode) next to the search bar; at most one active at a time, state persisted in localStorage['akasha-mode']. Each button enabled only when /api/integrations reports its tool ready; disabled buttons explain what is missing and offer the fix. Settings gains an Integrations block: per-tool statuses, Exa key entry that POSTs to the server (never stored client-side, round-trips as a boolean), agent permission mode selector, and consent states.

verification.command: npm run typecheck && npm run build
verification.expected: Typecheck and production build pass. Manual checklist (deferred review): AE1/AE5 disabled states with tooltips, mode exclusivity, key save round-trip shows boolean in GET /api/integrations

features.context_note: Follow #filters-btn/#settings-btn toggle pattern and akasha-* localStorage namespace; menubar click-outside gotcha: global handler closes menus only when click lands outside any .menu. Frontend stays framework-free imperative (KTD9).
features.evidence: typecheck + vite build green, 32/32 tests still pass. DEFERRED HUMAN REVIEW: M1 manual checklist (AE1/AE5 tooltips, mode exclusivity, key round-trip) pending — browser smoke planned after F004
features.verified_by: harness-progress verify (npm run typecheck && npm run build, exit 0)

features.behavior: qmd bridge on the server: GET /api/related?id= and GET /api/semantic-search?q= spawn 'qmd vsearch --format json' (never 'qmd query' or 'qmd search'), map result file paths to graph node ids and drop non-nodes, and accept a collections filter. Coverage detection via 'qmd collection list/show' against the vault root reuses existing covering collections. POST /api/qmd/setup creates the collection then runs 'qmd update' and 'qmd embed', reporting ready only when embeddings exist; GET /api/qmd/status reports missing/uncovered/indexing/ready. qmd binary missing yields a clean 503; malformed qmd JSON yields empty results plus a logged warning. Related queries use the note's title plus an excerpt as query text.

verification.expected: server/integrations/qmd.test.ts passes with a faked qmd runner: path->node-id mapping drops out-of-graph files (AE3), coverage detected for exact and parent-path roots (AE2), setup skipped when covered, status stays indexing until embeddings done (AE4), vsearch invoked not query/search, collections filter narrows, missing binary -> 503, malformed JSON -> empty + warning

features.context_note: Test-first with the injectable runner from F001; never invoke the real qmd binary in tests. vsearch chosen over query (too slow, LLM rerank) to meet the 1-2s target. Manual latency validation on a real vault happens at review.
features.evidence: npm test 46/46 + typecheck green. Real-vault check: /api/related on FeloVault (4049 notes) returns high-quality in-graph results reusing 9 existing collections. Latency: 7-9s/query (qmd per-spawn model+index load), NOT the 1-2s aspiration; vec:-typed queries avoid 30s+ LLM expansion; upgrade path = warm qmd mcp child (docs/solutions/qmd-vsearch-latency.md)
features.verified_by: harness-progress verify (npm test && npm run typecheck, exit 0) + real-vault probe

features.behavior: Semantic surfaces in the UI: every note opened in the reader ends with a 'Related notes (semantic)' section appended inside openReader() after the body renders, loading async with distinct loading/indexing/empty/error states; result clicks call select(n) to open the note and fly the camera. Rendered note HTML is sanitized with DOMPurify before innerHTML insertion (KTD13). Filters panel gains a Collections group with per-collection toggles persisted in localStorage['akasha-collections'] scoping all qmd surfaces. With Semantic mode active the search box swaps its content-search source to /api/semantic-search reusing addResult() rows. One-time setup prompt fires when status is qmd-present-without-coverage; declining leaves an 'Enable semantic search' action in Integrations settings.

verification.command: npm run typecheck && npm run build && npm test
verification.expected: Typecheck, build, and existing test suites pass (DOMPurify added as dependency). Manual checklist (deferred review): F1 and F2 flows on a real vault, collection toggle changes related results, indexing state during first index (AE4)

features.context_note: Follow openReader() loading-then-replace, renderFilters() imperative rows, and the search debounce + dedup pattern. Error state must be distinct from empty so a qmd 503 does not read as 'no related notes'.
features.evidence: typecheck+build+46 tests green. Playwright smoke on real vault: F2 end-to-end (search -> note -> related loads async -> 8 rows -> click swaps note + flies camera), collections group renders 9 toggles, semantic mode hits /api/semantic-search (network log), AE5 tooltip on disabled Web button, DOMPurify sanitizes reader HTML. Screenshot in session scratchpad. DEFERRED HUMAN REVIEW: F1 fresh-vault prompt flow + AE1 (qmd-absent) covered by unit tests only

features.behavior: Shared topology/gaps server module computing from the in-memory graph: phantom nodes with their inbound linkers, orphans (in + out === 0), and sparse clusters via a cheap degree/component heuristic. GET /api/gaps returns template-based suggested queries from phantom/orphan titles, enriched with a qmd snippet when available, capped at five; recomputed on graph reload. Consumed later by both Web-mode suggestions and agent context.

verification.expected: server/integrations/topology.test.ts passes: fixture graph yields expected phantoms with linker context, orphans detected, well-connected fixture yields no false gaps, suggestions capped at five, templates still produced when qmd enrichment absent

features.context_note: KTD6: one shared module, no LLM. Graph shape: node id = vault-relative path, phantom ids are 'phantom:' + lowercased target, NodeRec.in/out support orphan derivation (scanner/scan.ts).
features.evidence: npm test 53/53 + typecheck green. Real vault: /api/gaps ranks 1082 phantoms/2904 orphans into 5 sensible suggestions with linker context. Deviation noted: qmd snippet enrichment replaced by free linker-title context (per-suggestion vsearch would cost 25-45s at activation)
features.verified_by: harness-progress verify (npm test && npm run typecheck, exit 0) + real-vault probe

features.behavior: Server-side Exa adapter and POST /api/research endpoint: thin adapter isolating the exa-js request shape with defaults type:auto and highlights-only contents, optional deep-mode flag, retry/backoff on transient failures. Endpoint rejects with an actionable 4xx when no key is configured and with 403 (no outbound call) when Web-mode consent is absent from config. Responses never echo the key. exa-js pinned in package.json.

verification.expected: server/integrations/exa.test.ts passes: no-consent -> 403 with zero outbound calls (AE8 server side), missing key -> 4xx actionable, canned Exa response maps to UI shape, key never in response, transient failure retries then clean error

features.context_note: KTD4: Exa renamed request params twice in 12 months, keep its shape behind the adapter only. Fetch current exa-js docs before wiring (never assume from training data). Consent + key live in F001's config module.
features.evidence: npm test 62/62 + typecheck green. Grounded against exa-js dist types (installed pinned): type auto + highlights-only default, deep opt-in; no-consent -> 403 with zero outbound calls; missing key -> 400 actionable; 429/5xx retried with backoff, 401 not retried; key never echoed. DEFERRED: one live query with Felo's real key at review
features.verified_by: harness-progress verify (npm test && npm run typecheck, exit 0)

features.behavior: The single sanctioned vault-write path: POST /api/notes (create) and PUT /api/notes (edit) reusing the /api/note confinement logic (resolve under vault root + sep, .md only, phantom: rejected). Creates default to inbox/ under the scanned root (configurable destination); filename collisions get a suffix, never an overwrite, unless the request is an explicit edit of that path. Edits apply full-content replacement. Every write appends a change-log entry in the local data dir. Requests without the session token are rejected. Vault root missing fails cleanly.

verification.expected: server/integrations/write.test.ts passes every negative path: ../ and absolute and symlink-style ids rejected, non-.md rejected, missing token rejected, create lands in inbox/ by default and configured destination when set, create never overwrites, edit modifies only target, each write journals, missing vault root clean failure

features.context_note: Security-sensitive unit, test-first mandatory. Mirror the existing guard in server/app.ts /api/note; tests follow server/app.test.ts supertest temp-dir vault pattern.
features.evidence: npm test 74/74 + typecheck green. Negative paths all rejected: ../, absolute, phantom:, non-md, nested traversal, symlink-dir escape (realpath ancestor check), tokenless. Create defaults to inbox/ with collision suffixes, never overwrites; edit 404s instead of creating; every write journals to data/changes.jsonl; missing vault -> clean 503
features.verified_by: harness-progress verify (npm test && npm run typecheck, exit 0)

features.behavior: Web mode UI: first activation renders a consent modal disclosing egress (accept persists via server, decline leaves the mode off, AE8). Active mode shows suggested queries from /api/gaps above the search box (populate-then-confirm, no auto-spend), results in a panel, and a per-result save action that POSTs to /api/notes then triggers the existing rescan flow so the new node appears. Named states: loading indicator while /api/gaps and /api/research are in flight, inline error surfacing missing-key and retry-exhausted failures, empty-suggestions message for well-connected vaults.

verification.command: npm run typecheck && npm run build
verification.expected: Typecheck and build pass. Manual checklist (deferred review): full F3 flow on a real vault ending with the new node visible; AE8 consent gate before any request leaves the machine

features.context_note: Follow existing modal/panel toggling and the mi-rescan rescan-then-reload flow. Suggested query click populates an editable field, never auto-runs (spends Exa credit).
features.evidence: typecheck+build+74 tests green. Playwright F3 on a scratch vault: consent modal on first activation, decline leaves mode off with zero egress (AE8), accept persists and opens panel, gap suggestions from live topology, suggestion click populates (never auto-spends), mocked Exa results render, save-as-note lands in inbox/, rescan shows the new node (3->4). Inline error verified with invalid key (401 -> clean message). Global config restored after test. DEFERRED: live Exa query with real key

features.behavior: OpenCode bridge: spawn 'opencode serve' on 127.0.0.1 with a generated OPENCODE_SERVER_PASSWORD scoped to the vault directory (verify dir flag vs cwd against opencode serve --help first); generate a locked-down permission config denying edit/bash/webfetch/websearch/task/skill in BOTH modes with read glob-scoped to the vault root. Talk via @opencode-ai/sdk (pinned exact): POST /api/agent/session, POST /api/agent/message, GET /api/agent/stream (SSE relay of async prompt events), POST /api/agent/cancel, GET /api/agent/status (not-installed / installed-not-connected / ready). Session and message endpoints reject without recorded Agent-mode consent, no call to OpenCode. Child process restarts after crash with capped backoff. Agent context seeded with topology summary and qmd availability. Password never sent to the browser.

verification.expected: server/integrations/opencode.test.ts passes: permission config denies edit/bash/webfetch/websearch/task/skill in both modes and scopes read to vault root, out-of-vault read denied, no-consent requests rejected with zero OpenCode calls, crash restart with capped backoff, cancel tears down stream, status three-state, password set on spawn and absent from every response

features.context_note: KTD2/KTD3. Fetch current opencode.ai/docs/server + /sdk + /permissions before wiring (near-daily releases, pin exact version). Manual streamed-reply check with a Zen model happens at review.
features.evidence: npm test 86/86 + typecheck green. Lockdown profile denies edit/bash/webfetch/external_directory + disables write/edit/patch/bash/webfetch/websearch/task/skill tools in both modes; consent-gated session/message with zero OpenCode calls when refused; crash restart with capped injectable backoff; SSE relay filters by session; password never in responses. LIVE smoke against real opencode 1.17.13: spawn with OPENCODE_CONFIG_CONTENT lockdown, cwd=vault, basic-auth session create OK in 2.5s. Grounded: serve has no dir flag (cwd scoping), config injected via env, connected = auth.json entries
features.verified_by: harness-progress verify (npm test && npm run typecheck, exit 0) + live binary smoke

features.behavior: Proposal lifecycle and audit trail: agent creates (path+frontmatter+body) and edits (path+diff) are held server-side as proposals; GET /api/agent/proposals lists them, approve applies through the guarded write, reject discards leaving the vault untouched; edit-before-approve accepts modified content on approve. Full-access mode auto-approves each proposal but still applies through the same guarded write (no OpenCode-direct path) and emits an inline chat notice as writes land. Provenance frontmatter injected on agent-created notes; change log records actor, mode, path, timestamp for every applied change and survives server restart.

verification.expected: server/integrations/proposals.test.ts passes: reject -> vault untouched (AE6), approve create -> file with provenance frontmatter + journal entry, approve edit -> diff applied + journal (AE10), edit-before-approve persists modified body, no write path without a proposal id in approval mode, full-access write journals (AE9), journal survives restart

features.context_note: Test-first: this unit proves the trust model. Resolve the plan's open question here: capture proposals via ask-permission intercept or a custom propose tool; both must route through F007's endpoint.
features.evidence: npm test 101/101 + typecheck green. Open question resolved: proposals captured via a Solaris-generated OpenCode plugin (config.plugin path in data dir) registering propose_create/propose_edit; handlers POST back with a per-boot secret. Reject -> vault untouched (AE6); approve create -> provenance frontmatter + journal; edit -> diff preview + apply (AE10); edit-before-approve persists user body; full mode auto-applies through the SAME guarded write with mode:full journal (AE9); propose secret and browser token are non-interchangeable; journal is a plain file (survives restart)
features.verified_by: harness-progress verify (npm test && npm run typecheck, exit 0)

features.behavior: Agent chat UI: first activation walks the consent gate (disclosing egress and Zen training-data caveat) then Connect onboarding driven by /api/agent/status. Chat panel streams via SSE with a stop button and a connection-status banner when the stream drops during crash-restart backoff. Proposals render inline with body preview or diff and approve/edit/reject actions; full-access lands emit an inline write notice. Full-access toggle lives in settings behind an explicit standing-consent confirmation. Model selection reads from config, no hardcoded free model name.

verification.command: npm run typecheck && npm run build
verification.expected: Typecheck and build pass. Manual checklist (deferred review): F4 end-to-end (propose -> approve -> node + edge after rescan), F5 with full-access enabled, AE9/AE10

features.context_note: Follow existing panel patterns, reader-pane loading states, menubar click-outside gotcha for popovers. Diff rendering for edit proposals must be readable in the chat panel.
features.evidence: typecheck+build+101 tests green. Live browser smoke on scratch vault with REAL opencode: agent consent modal (egress + Zen training caveat), accept -> real locked-down serve spawn -> session created -> SSE stream open (reconnect banner wired to EventSource open/error). Full-access select guarded by standing-consent confirm; model input round-trips config. Proposal render/approve/edit/reject UI wired to the F010 routes (store paths fully unit-tested). DEFERRED HUMAN REVIEW: live F4/F5 with a real model turn (spends credits) — propose -> approve -> node+edge after rescan
features.verified_by: harness-progress verify (typecheck+build+test) + live browser smoke (no model spend)

features.behavior: Two-flavor install and trust-model docs: a CLI flag on bin/cli.ts plus a first-run UI prompt trigger POST /api/integrations/install; the installer checks existing installs first and never touches them (AE7), installs OpenCode via its official one-liner, installs qmd via Bun only when Bun exists, otherwise returns guided instructions instead of failing; install failures surface the tool's stderr cleanly. README.md and CLAUDE.md re-scope the 'nothing is uploaded' promise to the core and document the three modes, their consent gates, and the two install flavors.

verification.expected: server/integrations/install.test.ts passes with faked runners: existing qmd -> skip + config untouched (AE7), missing Bun -> guided instructions and no partial install, failure surfaces stderr, core behavior identical when addons declined. Docs updated consistently

features.context_note: KTD8/KTD11. Never run real installers in tests (faked runner). Fresh-machine dry-run of the prompt path is the deferred manual check.
features.evidence: npm test 107/107 + typecheck + build + build:cli green. installAddons checks existing installs first and runs zero installer commands when present (AE7); missing Bun -> guided instructions, no partial install; install failure surfaces stderr; opencode via official one-liner. POST /api/integrations/install token-guarded, invalidates detection cache; CLI --addons flag; settings 'install missing addons' button. README + CLAUDE.md re-scoped: core uploads nothing, Web/Agent consent-gated, single guarded write path documented. DEFERRED: fresh-machine dry-run

features.behavior: Integrations UI moves out of the settings panel (which returns to display-only) into the Tools menu as a mi-group, following the View-menu pattern: per-tool rows (qmd, Exa, OpenCode) with status, a per-tool 'install' button shown only when that tool is missing (extensible for future integrations like markitdown), Exa key entry, agent permission selector, re-check action, enable-semantic-search action. The install-all button is removed; per-tool installs call POST /api/integrations/install with tools:[name].

verification.command: npm run typecheck && npm run build && npm test
verification.expected: Gates green. Manual: settings panel shows only display controls; Tools menu carries integrations; dropdown stays open while interacting (click-outside gotcha preserved); install button visible only for missing tools

features.context_note: Menubar gotcha: global click handler closes menus only when click lands outside any .menu — controls inside the dropdown keep it open. Follow #mi-controls (View menu) structure.
features.evidence: typecheck+build+107 tests green. Browser check: Tools dropdown carries #mi-integrations, settings panel back to display-only, dropdown stays open on interaction, per-tool install buttons hidden when installed, statuses render (qmd 2.6.3, opencode 1.17.13, exa no key)

features.behavior: Agent model selector: GET /api/agent/models returns the OpenCode Zen provider's free models (cost.input+output === 0) from the running instance via client.config.providers(), plus the currently configured model. The Tools-menu integrations block gets a combobox: a select listing the free models plus a 'custom model…' option revealing a text input for provider/model entry; a typed value that matches a listed option selects it. Selection persists via defaultModel in config (no hardcoded model names).

verification.command: npm test && npm run typecheck && npm run build
verification.expected: Route test with fake opencode client returns only cost-0 zen models; typed custom value round-trips config; gates green

features.context_note: Zen = provider id 'opencode'. 5 free models live today; list is dynamic, never hardcode. Bridge must be running to list; fall back to the configured model + custom entry when agent is off.

features.behavior: Warm qmd client: Solaris keeps a persistent 'qmd mcp' child (JSON-RPC over stdio, initialize handshake, tools/call query with typed vec sub-queries and structuredContent parsing, collections scoping) with crash-restart, replacing per-query CLI spawns for /api/related and /api/semantic-search; CLI vsearch remains the fallback when the MCP boot fails. Plus a per-note related-notes cache keyed by note mtime + collections filter, invalidated on rescan/reload. Warm queries must return in under 1s (vs 5-9s spawn).

verification.expected: qmd-mcp tests with a faked stdio child: handshake, query call shape (vec-typed, collections), structuredContent mapping, crash-restart, fallback to CLI when boot fails; cache hit skips the query and rescan invalidates; real-vault probe shows warm latency under 1s

features.context_note: Verified live: qmd mcp 2.6.3, tools/call query {searches:[{type:'vec',...}], limit, collections, rerank:false} -> result.structuredContent.results [{docid,file:'collection/relpath',title,score,snippet}]. Warm call ~0.2s. Speak MCP protocolVersion 2025-06-18.

features.behavior: Progressive gap enrichment: suggestion rows render instantly from templates (as today), then each row's context is enriched asynchronously with a qmd snippet via GET /api/gaps/enrich?q= (using the warm qmd client; absent qmd -> no enrichment, templates stand alone). The UI updates each row as its snippet arrives; no blocking of panel open.

verification.command: npm test && npm run typecheck && npm run build
verification.expected: Enrich endpoint returns a snippet for a query via faked warm client and 204/empty when qmd missing; UI gates green; manual: rows fill in progressively

features.context_note: One warm query per suggestion (~0.2s each) fired in parallel from the browser; cap at the 5 rendered suggestions.

features.behavior: Fail-closed sandbox self-test: after spawning opencode serve, the bridge asserts the effective sandbox on the running server (config/tool surface must show edit/bash/webfetch/external_directory denied and write-shaped tools absent/disabled). On assertion failure the bridge marks itself unsafe, agent routes return 503 sandbox-unverified, and the child is stopped. Detection also records the last-validated opencode version and the status endpoint carries a driftWarning when the installed binary is newer.

verification.expected: Bridge test: fake client reporting a weakened sandbox -> ensureRunning rejects, child killed, agent session route 503s with sandbox-unverified; healthy fake passes; version drift sets driftWarning in /api/agent/status; live smoke against real opencode passes the self-test

features.context_note: Schema drift already happened once (websearch/task/skill permission keys removed). Fail closed: no assertion pass, no Agent mode. Check client.config.get() merged config and/or tool id list.
